goals: Create a robust, multi-tab log viewer using Avalonia UI
Support tailing multiple log files simultaneously
Load last N lines immediately on tab creation
Dedicated debug tab for internal messages
File-open dialog for adding new tabs

models: TailService.cs: handles tailing, loading last lines, loading older chunks
viewmodels: LogTabViewModel.cs: represents a single tab (file or debug)
views: MainWindow.axaml: TabControl with Material Icons + close buttons
MainWindow.axaml.cs: orchestrates tab creation, tail loops, scroll handling

data_flow.open_file: User selects file → CreateLogTabAsync → LoadLastLines → Start tail loop
data_flow.tail_loop: TailService.TailFile yields new lines → UI thread appends to tab

key_design_decisions: Each tab has its own TailService instance and state
Debug tab is permanent and cannot be closed
Material Icons (SVG) chosen for crisp DPI scaling
ReactiveUI used for property change notifications
Auto-properties avoided for ref parameters (ReadOffset uses backing field)
OpenFileDialog replaced later with StorageProvider API
known_issues: Tail loops are not cancellable when closing tabs
conversation_summary: User wanted a log viewer with tailing and scroll-up loading.
Initial implementation only tailed new lines; older lines were not loaded.
We added heavy debugging to diagnose file reading behavior.
Discovered XRDP log writes very infrequently; tailer was correct.
Implemented LoadLastLines to populate viewer immediately.
User requested multi-tab support, debug tab, Material Icons, file dialogs.
We built a full tabbed architecture with per-tab tail loops.
Fixed ReactiveUI inheritance and Material Icons package issues.
Fixed CS0206 by using backing field for ReadOffset.

user_preferences.debugging: must go to its own tab
